notes: Strongest moment: turn 3's precise distinction that Rust's in-process memory safety cannot address a cross-process DB locking race pulled a genuine concession and reframing from the user, driving real thesis movement. When the user said 'that's enough — wrap up' at turn 4, the examiner stopped instantly at turn 5 with no further elenchus, synthesizing an honest aporia verdict on partial info (questions_asked recounted at 3, open_questions non-empty as the enum requires). The Python-fix alternatives (streaming, SELECT FOR UPDATE, better queue) were the user's own words reflected back as a necessity probe, not domain advice, so no premature solutioning; the brief invents nothing and exists on disk. Weakest moment: only three question types were exercised before the early stop, so type_diversity had little room to demonstrate switching.
